title: Plectranthus scebeli
taxonomy: Plectranthus scebeli is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It is a member of the genus Plectranthus, which comprises a diverse group of plants. Plectranthus scebeli is further categorized into the subfamily Lamioideae. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics of the plant. The exact classification of Plectranthus scebeli may vary slightly depending on the source and the taxonomic system used.

In some regions, it is used to treat fever and other infections due to its antimicrobial properties. intro: Plectranthus scebeli, also known as Coleus scebeli, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it exhibits potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Plectranthus scebeli include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Plectranthus scebeli is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as respiratory infections, skin disorders, and digestive issues.
